shower.thought:
# 1
You're never actually late to anything—you're just early to something else, and everyone else decided when that something should start.

# 2
The moment you understand why someone did something terrible, you've accidentally made it slightly less terrible.

# 3
We call it "falling asleep" but we're actually just giving up consciousness for eight hours and hoping it works out.

# 4
Every person you'll never meet is living a full life right now with the same intensity and detail as yours, completely indifferent to your existence.

# 5
Confidence is just anxiety that's learned to look you in the eye.

# 6
You've already forgotten more things than most people will ever learn, and you'll never know what you're missing.

# 7
The people who talk the most about being real are often the ones performing the hardest.

# 8
Time doesn't move faster as you get older—you just have fewer new things to experience, so there's less to actually remember.

# 9
Grief is just love with nowhere to go.

# 10
You become cynical not because the world gets worse, but because you finally see it clearly.

# 11
The scariest part of growing up is realizing your parents were always just making it up as they went.

# 12
You'll spend most of your life trying to prove something to someone who's already forgotten they questioned you.

# 13
Loneliness isn't the absence of people—it's the absence of people who get it, and sometimes even they don't.

# 14
We document our lives so intensely now that we might forget to actually live them.

# 15
Your biggest fear probably isn't what you think it is—it's usually something you haven't named yet.

# 16
Success is just failure that happened to work out, dressed up and given a better story.

# 17
You're nostalgic for moments you didn't even appreciate while they were happening.

# 18
The people you're jealous of are usually jealous of someone too.

# 19
We teach kids to follow their passion, but passion without discipline is just expensive nostalgia waiting to happen.

# 20
You've probably given someone the exact advice they needed without realizing it, and never knew it changed their life.

motivational.thought: The only person who can pull you down is you, and you're better than that. — Unknown
Your future self is watching you right now through memories you're creating. Make them count. — Unknown
Comparison is the thief of contentment, but it's a terrible investment advisor too. — Unknown
You don't need permission to become who you're meant to be. — Unknown
The difference between who you are and who you want to be is what you do. — Bill Phillips
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Unknown
Every no gets you closer to the yes that matters. — Unknown
You can't change the direction of the wind, but you can adjust your sails. — Dolly Parton
The person who says it cannot be done should not interrupt the person doing it. — Chinese Proverb
Regret for things we did can be tempered by time; regret for things we did not do is inconsolable. — Sydney Smith
Your potential is endless, but your time is not. Choose wisely. — Unknown
What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Success is not final, failure is not fatal—it's the courage to continue that counts. — Winston Churchill
The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now. — Chinese Proverb
Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will. — Unknown
You are not obligated to finish what doesn't feed your soul. — Unknown
The only way out is through. — Robert Frost
Stop waiting for the perfect moment. The perfect moment is now, imperfect as it may be. — Unknown
Yesterday is history, tomorrow is mystery, but today is a gift. That's why it's called the present. — Bill Keane
Your only limit is you. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my friend he was self-sabotaging his relationships.
He said, "Finally, something I'm good at."

My therapist asked if I was having suicidal thoughts.
I said, "Only on weekdays."

I went to a support group for people with commitment issues.
Didn't go back.

My dentist said my teeth were rotting from the inside out.
I said, "Join the club."

I asked my dad why he never showed emotion.
He just stared at me for 20 minutes, which I guess answered that.

My friend said he was going to live his best life.
He's been on the couch for six months. I think he's nailing it.

I told my partner I needed space.
He bought me a studio apartment and left the keys on the table.
I think I communicated too clearly.

My mom asked if I was eating enough vegetables.
I said, "Does alcohol count as a vegetable?" 
She cried. I think I was being literal.

I started journaling for my mental health.
Turns out I have nothing interesting to say and I'm boring even to myself.

My coworker asked how I manage my anxiety.
I said, "I don't. I just let it manage me now."

I got fired for being too honest at work.
Well, that's what HR *said*. I think they just hated me.

My therapist went on vacation.
I've never felt more abandoned by someone I pay to care.

I told my friend he needed to let go of his past.
He said he was trying, but the charges won't stick.

My doctor said I needed to cut back on stress.
I said, "Doc, you're literally my biggest stressor."
He referred me to someone else. Guess I was right.

I asked my mom when she'd stop worrying about me.
She said, "Probably at your funeral." 
I said, "That's dark." 
She said, "You think that's dark? Wait till you see my browser history."

My friend said he was taking a break from dating.
He's been on that break for four years.
At this point, I think he's found his forever person: himself.

I started taking anti-anxiety medication.
Now I'm just anxious about what I'll do without it.
It's anxiety all the way down.

My Spotify Wrapped showed I'd listened to sad songs 847 times.
It recommended therapy.
Even my algorithm knows I'm fucked.

I told my boss I'd been struggling with depression.
He said, "Yeah, most people are when they work here."
Honestly, it was the most validating thing anyone's said to me.

My ex said she needed someone more emotionally available.
I said, "Fair." 
We haven't spoken since, which honestly proves her point.
